title: Pseudopiptocarpha tovarensis
intro: Pseudopiptocarpha tovarensis is a plant species native to the tropical regions of South America, belonging to the family Asteraceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments including wound healing and infections. The bioactive constituents of Pseudopiptocarpha tovarensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are consumed for their therapeutic effects.

It belongs to the genus Pseudopiptocarpha, which is a group of plants native to Central and South America. The genus Pseudopiptocarpha is part of the larger clade of flowering plants known as Angiosperms. Within the Asteraceae family, Pseudopiptocarpha tovarensis is further grouped with other related species in the subfamily Vernonioideae.
